key_findings: Three-phase 36h execution window: H0-H6 (SQLite durability foundation), H6-H18 (sink lifecycle hardening), H18-H36 (Pandavs parallel sink integration)
H18 Go-Gate is the single most critical checkpoint: SLO-1 (zero duplicate chunk executions) is an absolute blocker for Phase 3 entry — no exceptions under any operational pressure
H6 Go-Gate has 4 blocking criteria and 1 non-blocking; H18 has 6 blocking criteria; H36 has 6 blocking criteria — 'promote only on evidence' is the governing discipline
Every gate has an explicit rollback action mapped to individual failing criteria — operators have an unambiguous action for any failure mode without consulting the report
Hourly report schema is DB-derived and mandatory: chunks_done, coverage_pct, events_persisted, sink_lag_h, eta_h, blockers — sink_lag_h > 2h is an immediate blocker at any phase
sink_lag_h is the canary metric: if it exceeds 2.0h at any point during Phases 1-2, it is an immediate blocker requiring concurrency reduction before any gate evaluation
Phase 3 is fully additive: SQLite outbox sweep continues unchanged throughout Phase 3; Pandavs is added alongside, never replacing, until H36 gate confirms integrity
Tracker template (execution_tracker_template.md) is a fill-in-place operational document; gate evaluation sections cannot be marked PASS without all sub-criteria checked off
Critical path: Phase 1 = 5.5h, Phase 2 = 8.5h, Phase 3 = 11.5h, total = 25.5h on critical path; slack exists for non-critical-path tasks
Framework is reusable: Phase 2 backpressure/SLO patterns and Phase 3 gate criteria are pipeline-agnostic; replace only Phase 1 setup tasks for nuclei/enrichment lanes

phases.name: Zero-Loss Local Capture
phases.objective: Establish SQLite durability layer; all records in SQLite before any sink attempt

phases.name: Sink Connection + Queue Lifecycle
phases.objective: Connect live sinks, harden queue lifecycle, prove SLO-1 zero duplicate executions
phases.critical_task: T010 (SLO-1 drill) — must pass before any other Phase 2 gate criteria evaluated

phases.name: Pandavs Parallel Sink Integration
phases.objective: Add Pandavs as second parallel sink; verify integrity over 18h; prepare Path B promotion
phases.condition: GATE-H18 must PASS; otherwise Phase 3 deferred indefinitely

gates.rollback_if_fail: Do not start Phase 2; fix failing criterion; re-evaluate

gates.slo_blocker: SLO-1 (G2-C1): zero duplicate chunk executions — absolute blocker, no exceptions
gates.rollback_if_fail: Do not start Phase 3; if SLO-1 fails, mandatory rollback grant_lease() and revert to file-list dispatch
Rollback to Path A indefinitely; disable sink_target='pandavs'; schedule new 36h window after root cause resolved

hourly_report_schema.canary_action: Immediate blocker at any phase; reduce concurrency 50%; alert operator
hourly_report_schema.prohibited: File-system counts must never appear in any hourly report field

risk_model.title: SLO-1 drill reveals duplicate chunk execution
risk_model.mitigation: Mandatory stop; rollback grant_lease(); revert to file-list dispatch; audit chunk_queue before retry

risk_model.title: T013 schema incompatibility blocks Phase 3
risk_model.mitigation: Abort Phase 3; maintain Path A indefinitely; schema gap must be resolved in separate window

risk_model.title: sink_lag_h exceeds 2h during Phase 2
risk_model.mitigation: Immediate: reduce concurrency 50%; investigate sink write bottleneck; do not proceed to GATE-H18 until resolved

risk_model.title: Row count delta > 1% in T015
risk_model.mitigation: Pause dual-write; audit missing records; identify Pandavs ingest failure mode; do not proceed to T016

risk_model.title: MTTR drill fails (> 15 min) in T017
risk_model.mitigation: Do not promote; optimize restart sequence; extend Phase 3 window if feasible

risk_model.title: Task schedule drift causes gate slip
risk_model.mitigation: Hourly ETA field in progress report catches drift early; operator escalation protocol at 2h behind schedule

risk_model.title: Non-monotonic hourly report during Phase 2
risk_model.mitigation: Investigate immediately; block gate evaluation until root cause found; never accept non-monotonic as 'acceptable'

recommended_actions.action: Execute T001-T005 in strict dependency order; do not parallelise steps within critical path — each depends on the previous
Capture master status query baseline at H1 (T006) — provides ground truth for all subsequent hourly comparisons
Run outbox sweep T007 in dry-run mode first; review log before enabling live writes in T011 — never skip dry-run validation
Treat sink_lag_h > 2h as a page-level alert at any hour; it gates GATE-H18 and is the canary for sink health
SLO-1 drill at T010: run the SQL query and record the exact row count — do not proceed to T011 with non-zero result under any operational pressure
At H18 gate: verify all 6 criteria before allowing any Phase 3 task to begin — no exceptions for 'we'll fix it in Phase 3'
T013 is a hard abort point: if schema or runtime validation fails, stop Phase 3, document the gap, maintain Path A — do not attempt T014 with unresolved compatibility issues
Run T017 (MTTR) and T018 (rollback test) before H36 gate evaluation — gate cannot pass without both drills complete and logged

assumptions: A1: scan_persistence.db is accessible throughout all 36h; no planned downtime
A2: Active scanning continues throughout; no scan pause longer than heartbeat interval
A3: Hourly report generation can be automated via cron querying scan_persistence.db
A4: Pandavs module available on same host or LAN for T013 validation (no internet dependency)
A5: ClickHouse/Neo4j credentials available for live sink writes in T011
A6: All schema changes are additive; non-destructive rule enforced throughout
A7: 51-chunk run will not complete before H18 (scan will still be in progress during GATE-H18 evaluation)
